description: Visual UX polish pass with browser-verified fixes. Every change must be visually verified via the browser test helper before committing.

userStories.title: Fix double-click text creation positioning — text appears outside document
userStories.description: As a user, I want double-clicking the canvas to create text AT the click position within the document, not above it.
acceptanceCriteria: In use-canvas-interactions.ts, the double-click-to-create-text code at the empty canvas branch passes correct world coordinates to addTextLayer
The text layer should appear centered on where the user double-clicked
Verify by running: browser-test.ts create-text — the text should be visible on the white document area, not above it
The Y coordinate in properties panel should be a reasonable number (not -500+)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: clamped text position to document bounds with padding, fixed text tool click to use click position, and made properties panel show document-relative coordinates.

userStories.title: Fix SVG overlay intercepting canvas pointer events
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click/double-click on the canvas without SVG overlays blocking my clicks.
acceptanceCriteria: All SVG overlays (SelectionOutline, TransformHandles, DrawPreviewOverlay, InlineTextEditor bounding box) must have pointer-events:none on the SVG container
Individual interactive elements within TransformHandles (the draggable handle rects) should have pointer-events:auto
Non-interactive elements (selection outline polygon, bounding box, selection highlights) must have pointer-events:none
Verify by running: browser-test.ts investigate — double-click to edit text should work without { force: true }
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: hidden TransformHandles during text editing, added double-click forwarding in handles, explicit pointer-events=none on non-interactive elements.

userStories.title: Fix text layer thumbnail — shows garbled text, needs proper scaling
userStories.description: As a user, I want text layer thumbnails in the layers panel to show a readable preview of the text.
acceptanceCriteria: Text thumbnail should show the first few characters of text content clearly readable in 32x32
For short text, scale to fit the width. For long text, clip with ellipsis or just show beginning
Background should contrast with text color (light bg for dark text, dark bg for light text)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts investigate — check screenshot 04-layers-tab for readable thumbnails
Empty text layers show the Type icon, not blank canvas
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: custom text thumbnail rendering at 11px with clipping and contrast background.

userStories.title: Add selection outline to selected layers on canvas
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see which layer is selected on the canvas with a clear outline.
acceptanceCriteria: When a layer is selected, show a blue outline around its bounds on the canvas
The outline should be visible regardless of zoom level (stroke width adjusts for zoom)
The outline should NOT appear during inline text editing (the bounding box handles that)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts create-shape — selected rectangle should have a visible outline
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: increased stroke width/opacity, hidden during text editing.

userStories.title: Improve toolbar visual hierarchy and spacing
userStories.description: As a user, I want the toolbar to clearly show tool groups and the active tool.
acceptanceCriteria: Active tool has a stronger visual indicator — solid blue background, not subtle
DRAW section label is more visible (current DRAW text is very faint)
Add a visual separator between pointer tools (pointer/hand/zoom/crop) and draw tools (rect/ellipse/text)
Add a visual separator between draw tools and action buttons (group/save/undo/redo)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts screenshot — toolbar should have clear visual grouping
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: solid blue active tool, visible DRAW label, reorganized button groups.

userStories.title: Fix canvas area not filling available space on initial load
userStories.description: As a user, I want the document to be centered and fit the available canvas area on first load.
acceptanceCriteria: After creating a new canvas or loading a document, call fitToDocument to center and zoom the document to fit
The document should be visible with some padding around it, not cropped or too small
Verify by running: browser-test.ts screenshot — the white document should be nicely centered with visible checkerboard around all edges
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Fixed: double rAF ensures layout is settled before fitToDocument.

userStories.title: Add empty state for adjust panel when no image layer is selected
userStories.description: As a user, I want the adjust panel to tell me what to do when no image layer is selected.
acceptanceCriteria: When no layer is selected, show 'Select an image layer to adjust'
When a non-image layer is selected (text, shape, group), show 'Adjustments are only available for image layers'
When an image layer is selected, show the adjustment sliders (current behavior)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts investigate — check screenshot 06-adjust-tab
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Need to check what the adjust panel currently shows for non-image layers. It should have helpful empty states.

userStories.title: Fix new canvas dialog — preview shows wrong aspect ratio
userStories.description: As a user, I want the new canvas preview to accurately represent the document dimensions I chose.
acceptanceCriteria: The preview rectangle in the new canvas dialog should match the aspect ratio of the selected dimensions
When dimensions change, the preview updates immediately
Standard presets show correct preview (1080x1080 = square, 1080x1920 = tall portrait, etc.)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts screenshot (on the initial new canvas screen)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: From the initial screenshot, the preview area shows a white rectangle that doesn't match the 1920x1080 aspect ratio well.

userStories.title: Add loading state for WASM initialization
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see a loading indicator while WASM initializes, not a blank or broken screen.
acceptanceCriteria: Show a spinner or loading text while wasmReady is false
The loading state should be in App.tsx or EditorLayout, gating the editor behind wasmReady
Once WASM is ready, show the editor immediately without flash
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: WASM initialization is async. If it takes time, the editor could show without WASM features working. Check App.tsx for how wasmReady is used.

userStories.title: Fix properties panel input focus stealing keyboard shortcuts
userStories.description: As a user, I want keyboard shortcuts to not trigger while I'm typing in a properties panel input.
acceptanceCriteria: When a properties panel input is focused, typing 'r' should NOT switch to rectangle tool
When a properties panel input is focused, typing 'v' should NOT switch to pointer tool
In use-keyboard-shortcuts.ts, verify the tag check (INPUT/TEXTAREA/SELECT) properly guards ALL shortcuts
Also check that contentEditable elements are excluded
Verify: focus a numeric input in props panel, type numbers — only the input should change, not the tool
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The tag check exists but might have edge cases. Some shortcuts may not be properly guarded. This is critical for usability — accidentally switching tools while editing a property value is very frustrating.

userStories.title: Add drag-to-reorder layers in layers panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag layers to reorder them in the layers panel.
acceptanceCriteria: Each layer item in the layers panel should be draggable
Show a visual indicator (blue line between items) for the drop position
Dropping reorders via store.reorderLayers(layerId, newIndex)
Dragging should work for root-level layers (groups are stretch goal)
Verify by running: browser-test.ts investigate — create multiple layers and try reordering
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The store has reorderLayers() but the UI has no drag-to-reorder. This is essential for a production editor. Use HTML5 drag and drop or pointer events.

userStories.title: Add confirmation dialog for destructive actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want a confirmation before deleting the last layer or clearing the canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Deleting the last remaining layer shows a confirmation dialog
The confirm dialog uses the existing ConfirmDialog component at components/dialogs/confirm-dialog.tsx
Deleting a non-last layer does NOT show confirmation (would be annoying)
Undo is available after deletion (already works via history)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Currently Delete immediately removes the layer with no confirmation. Undo exists but a confirm for the last layer prevents accidental empty state.
